title: Small-cap healthcare stocks with A+ grade EPS revisions ahead of earnings
content:
[Health Care Medical Stock Market Industry Sector Wall Street Buildings 3d Illustration]
iQoncept/iStock via Getty Images

As Q1 2026 earnings season approaches, the healthcare sector enters reporting season as one of the market's primary laggards, with the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XLV]) down 5.29% in Q1 2026, slightly underperforming the S&P 500's 4.63% decline amid inflation pressures, rising Treasury yields, and tighter Medicare Advantage reimbursement conditions. 

Against that challenging backdrop, upward EPS revisions within the small-cap healthcare space stand out as a particularly meaningful signal, indicating that analysts see company-specific catalysts strong enough to override sector-wide headwinds. 

The following list highlights small-cap healthcare stocks that have earned the highest possible Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Grade of A+, spanning biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, health care services, and health care equipment.

The list includes Design Therapeutics, Inc. (DSGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DSGN]), Immuneering Corporation (IMRX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMRX]), and InnovAge Holding Corp. (INNV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INNV]), all carrying the highest A+ EPS revision grade. InnovAge Holding Corp. (INNV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INNV]) stands out with a Strong Buy quant rating of 4.87, while KalVista Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (KALV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KALV]) follows closely with a Strong Buy rating of 4.66.

The list features diversity across healthcare subsectors, including biotechnology firms like Kyverna Therapeutics, Inc. (KYTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KYTX]) and vTv Therapeutics Inc. (VTVT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTVT]), as well as health care equipment providers such as Pulse Biosciences, Inc. (PLSE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLSE]) and Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. (TCMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TCMD]). Theravance Biopharma, Inc. (TBPH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TBPH]) represents the pharmaceuticals industry with a Strong Buy rating of 4.56.

title: 3 Medical Instrument Stocks Tapping GenAI to Navigate Industry Woes
content:
Over the past 12 months, the application of generative AI (GenAI) in the Medical Instruments industry has moved from experimental to operational use, enhancing diagnostics, patient monitoring and intervention workflows. GenAI could transform health care through synthetic medical imaging, disease progression modeling sand drug molecule design with simulated effect, according to a 2025 U.S. National Science Foundation report. At the same time, regulatory bodies are adapting. The European Union AI Act (EU AI Act) 2024 introduced strict requirements for high-risk AI systems, such as AI-based software for medical purposes, including risk-mitigation systems, high-quality data sets and clear user information. In a recent article published in JAMA Health Forum, experts have proposed an approach to overseeing the clinical use of GenAI, comparable to how human clinicians are prepared and evaluated.

According to Fortune Business Insights, the global AI in the healthcare market is valued at $39.34 billion in 2025, and is projected to see a CAGR of 44% through 2034. Even so, challenges such as high implementation expenses, data breach concerns, and broader macroeconomic pressures weigh on the MedTech sector. 

Companies like Globus Medical GMED, Tactile Systems Technology TCMD and IRadimed Corp. IRMD have adapted well to changing consumer preferences.

Industry Description

The Zacks Medical - Instruments industry is highly fragmented, with participants engaged in research and development (R&D) in therapeutic areas. This FDA-regulated sector encompasses a vast array of products, from transcatheter valves and orthopedic devices to advanced imaging equipment and robotics. Recent trends highlight the integration of AI in diagnostics, the expansion of telemedicine, the rise of robotic-assisted surgeries and developments in 3D printing, continuous glucose monitoring systems and gene editing. The rise of GenAI is also reshaping MedTech, from speeding up patient recruitment to optimizing trial designs and improving regulatory processes. The FDA’s Total Product Life Cycle approach supports faster development of safe and effective medical devices critical to public health.

3 Trends Shaping the Future of the Medical Instruments Industry

GenAI Revolution: Over the past couple of years, there has been a significant increase in the adoption of GenAI within the medical instrument space, with hyper-personalization being the primary feature of GenAI-driven treatment options. GenAI, while analyzing vast and complex genetic and molecular data, is expected to help healthcare reach new heights in terms of predictive treatment options and smart hospital systems. According to the latest analysis by Towards Healthcare, global GenAI in the healthcare market is valued at $2.65 billion in 2025 and projected to expand at a CAGR of 35.1% through 2035. Key factors fueling the market’s growth include the increasing adoption of AI in healthcare, the growing availability of large healthcare datasets, and the need for more efficient and accurate decision-making tools. The application of AI in the diagnostics space is growing enormously, with the market expected to witness a CAGR of 46.1% by 2034.

Story Continues

M&A Trend: The medical instrument space has been benefiting from the ongoing merger and acquisition (M&A) trend. It is a known fact that smaller and mid-sized industry players attempt to compete with the big shots through consolidation. The big players attempt to enter new markets through a niche product. According to a Bain & Company report, momentum in medtech M&A rebounded in 2025 and continued to be dominated by smaller, targeted transactions. Larger deals such as Waters’ acquisition of BD’s Biosciences and Diagnostics businesses, and Stryker’s acquisition of Inari Medical grabbed headlines. Notably, spin-offs and divestitures accounted for more than a third of strategic deal value in the first 11 months of 2025, up from the previous five-year average. The report projected dealmaking strength to continue in 2026, as already seen with Boston Scientific’s $14.5-billion agreement to acquire Penumbra and Danaher’s $9.9-billion definitive agreement to acquire Masimo Corp.

Business Trend Disruption: Per the IMF’s January 2026 World Economic Outlook, global growth is expected to be steady at 3.3% in 2026 and 3.2% in 2027, revised slightly up since the October 2025 report. A few countries, especially low-income developing countries, have seen sizable downside growth revisions, often as a result of increased conflicts and recent tariff shocks. The good news is that global headline inflation is expected to decline from an estimated 4.1% in 2025 to 3.8% in 2026 and to 3.4% in 2027. However, the IMF apprehends that the current policy-generated disruptions to the ongoing disinflation process could interrupt the pivot to easing monetary policy, with implications for fiscal sustainability and financial stability. There are chances of higher nominal wage growth, which, in some cases, reflects the catch-up of real wages, accompanied by weak productivity and could make it difficult for firms to moderate price increases, especially when profit margins are already squeezed.

Zacks Industry Rank Indicates Bright Prospects

The Zacks Medical Instruments industry’s Zacks Industry Rank, which is basically the average of the Zacks Rank of all the member stocks, indicates upbeat near-term prospects. The industry, housed within the broader Zacks Medical sector, currently carries a Zacks Industry Rank #87, which places it in the top 36% of 244 Zacks industries. Our research shows that the top 50% of the Zacks-ranked industries outperform the bottom 50% by a factor of more than 2 to 1.

We will present a few stocks that have the potential to outperform the market based on a strong earnings outlook. It is worth taking a look at the industry’s shareholder returns and current valuation first.

Industry Underperforms S&P 500 & Sector

The industry has underperformed the Zacks S&P 500 composite and the sector in the past year.

The industry has declined 9% compared with the broader sector’s fall of 6.4%. The S&P 500 has returned 15.8% in a year.

1-Year Price PerformanceZacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Industry's Current Valuation

On the basis of the forward 12-month price-to-earnings (P/E), which is commonly used for valuing medical stocks, the industry is currently trading at 25.23X compared with the broader industry’s 19.33X and the S&P 500’s 20.89X.

Over the past five years, the industry has traded as high as 31.10X, as low as 25.23X and at the median of 28.77X, as the charts show below.

Price-to-Earnings Forward 12 Months (F12M)Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Price-to-Earnings Forward 12 Months (F12M)Zacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

3 Stocks to Buy Right Now

Globus Medical: The company offers a comprehensive portfolio of differentiated technologies used to treat a variety of musculoskeletal conditions.Globus Medical expanded its global reach with the NuVasive merger in 2023, while the 2025 Nevro Merger gave the potential to alter the standard of care in the neuromodulation space and beyond. As of Dec. 31, 2025, it had an existing direct or distributor sales presence in 64 countries outside the United States.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) company’s 2026 EPS indicates an 8.7% rise from that reported in 2025. The consensus mark for 2026 revenues implies a 11.8% improvement. Globus Medical has an earnings yield of 5.2% against the industry’s negative 1.6% yield.

You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

Price & Consensus: GMEDZacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Tactile Systems Technology: The company provides medical devices for the treatment of underserved chronic diseases. Tactile Systems’ therapeutic focus spans vascular disease, including lymphedema and venous insufficiency, alongside oncology and airway clearance therapy for respiratory conditions. The acquisition of LymphaTech Inc. in February 2026 is viewed as a milestone in Tactile Systems’ evolution from a product-based company to a comprehensive, integrated solutions provider for lymphatic dysfunction.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) company’s 2026 EPS calls for 9.1% growth. The consensus mark for 2026 revenues indicates a 39% improvement. Tactile Systems has an earnings yield of 4.4% against the industry’s negative 1.6% yield.

Price & Consensus: TCMDZacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

IRadimed: The company manufactures and markets Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)-compatible medical devices and related accessories, disposables and services. It is the only known provider of a non-magnetic intravenous infusion pump system designed for safe use during MRI procedures and operates dependably in magnetic fields up to 15,000 gauss. In the United States, the company has a direct sales strategy, while internationally, it serves roughly 80 countries via independent distributors.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for this Zacks Rank #2 company’s 2026 EPS implies year-over-year growth of 10.8%. The consensus mark for 2026 revenues indicates an improvement of 8.8%. iRadimed has an earnings yield of 2.1% against the industry’s negative 1.6% yield.

Price & Consensus: IRMDZacks Investment Research

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

title: UFP Technologies, Inc. (UFPT): A Bull Case Theory
content:
We came across a bullish thesis on UFP Technologies, Inc. on X.com by @MoneyShow. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on UFPT. UFP Technologies, Inc.'s share was trading at $215.67 as of February 26th. UFPT’s trailing P/E was 22.68 according to Yahoo Finance.Tactile Systems Technology (TCMD) Reports 12% Revenue Growth to $329.5M for 2025

A medical technician holding the instruments in her hands

UFP Technologies, Inc. designs and manufactures solutions for medical devices, sterile packaging, and other engineered custom products in the United States. UFPT is positioned as a high-quality growth investment operating at the intersection of engineered materials and mission-critical medical device manufacturing. The company has carved out a defensible niche as a custom development and manufacturing organization (CDMO), serving high-growth medical segments such as single-use devices, sterile packaging, and robotic surgery, the latter expanding at a mid-teens CAGR.

Its role as an outsource partner to 26 of the top 30 global medical device manufacturers underpins sticky, predictable revenue streams and long-term visibility. Financial performance reflects this strength: UFP surpassed $500 million in sales for the first time in 2024, while delivering 15.1% annualized revenue growth and 24.9% EPS growth over the past decade. Momentum has accelerated further, with Q2 2025 revenue rising 37.2% year over year.

Operational execution remains a differentiator, as evidenced by a 29.1% gross margin in 2024, above its five-year average, supported by a low-cost country manufacturing footprint and disciplined acquisition integration. The balance sheet is equally compelling, with debt at 32% of total capital and trending downward, providing flexibility to pursue strategic acquisitions and target 15% annual EPS growth over the next five years.

At current levels, the stock trades in an attractive Buy Zone, offering a projected 16.5% total annual return and a favorable 3.1-to-1 upside-to-downside ratio. The combination of resilient end markets, disciplined capital allocation, and accelerating growth supports a strong long-term investment case.

Previously, we covered a bullish thesis on West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. (WST) by Swiss Transparent Portfolio in May 2025, highlighting its durable leadership in injectable drug packaging and strong operational execution despite short-term earnings softness. WST’s stock has appreciated approximately 19.43% since coverage. @MoneyShow shares a similar view but emphasizes UFP Technologies’ (UFPT) high-growth medical device niche and resilient, compounding revenue streams.

Story Continues

UFP Technologies, Inc. is not on our list of the 30 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds. As per our database, 25 hedge fund portfolios held UFPT at the end of the third quarter which was 25 in the previous quarter. While we acknowledge the risk and potential of UFPT as an investment, our conviction lies in the belief that some AI stocks hold greater promise for delivering higher returns and doing so within a shorter time frame. If you are looking for an AI stock that is more promising than UFPT and that has 10,000% upside potential, check out our report about this cheapest AI stock.

title: Tactile Systems Technology (TCMD) Reports 12% Revenue Growth to $329.5M for 2025
content:
Tactile Systems Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:TCMD) is one of the best stocks for beginners with little money in 2026. On February 17, Tactile Systems Technology delivered financial results for 2025, with total revenue rising 12% to $329.5 million. The company concluded the year with a robust cash position of $83.4 million.

During this period, the company acquired Lymphotec to target the large population of undiagnosed lymphedema patients. By integrating Lymphotec’s fluid-monitoring tech into its commercial engine, Tactile aims to enhance patient surveillance and streamline the path to therapy. The CEO noted that 2024 investments in a new CRM system and expanded sales headcount are now driving productivity gains. Additionally, updated Medicare National Coverage Determination criteria have provided a tailwind by allowing certain patients to access the FlexiTouch system more directly.Tactile Systems Technology (TCMD) Reports 12% Revenue Growth to $329.5M for 2025

A medical technician holding the instruments in her hands

Despite the positive momentum, Tactile Systems Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:TCMD) issued a balanced outlook for 2026, projecting revenue growth of 8% to 11%. This guidance accounts for a potential short-term headwind: a new Medicare prior authorization requirement for pneumatic compression devices.

Tactile Systems Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:TCMD) is a medical technology company that develops and provides medical devices to treat underserved chronic diseases in the US.

While we acknowledge the potential of TCMD as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Why Tactile Systems Technology Stock Surged by Nearly 18% Today
content:
Next-generation medical device company Tactile Systems Technology(NASDAQ: TCMD) was the very picture of health on Wednesday, at least as far as its stock was concerned. Investors bid up the company's shares robustly, by nearly 18%, thanks to an impressive earnings report.

Healthy increases

Just after market close on Tuesday, Tactile published its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 figures. The company's total revenue rose by a sturdy 21% to $103.6 million, and net income in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) increased by 9% to $10.6 million ($0.46 per share).

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »Image source: Getty Images.

Both headline numbers beat the average analyst estimates. Prognosticators following Tactile were collectively expecting the company to post revenue below $94 million and per-share profit of $0.44.

Tactile attributed the considerable revenue gain, in part, to a 66% increase in sales of its airway clearance products. It also recorded a 16% rise in both sales and rentals of its devices for treating lymphedema, a disorder of the body's lymphatic system.

Good guidance

Investors were also cheered by Tactile's rather bullish guidance for the entirety of 2026. Management is expecting revenue to grow by 8% to 11% over the 2025 figure, specifically to $357 million to $365 million. This should shake out into a non-GAAP (adjusted) earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) number between $49 million $51 million. This would compare favorably to 2025's $44.8 million.

I see almost nothing to dislike in both Tactile's quarter and its full-year 2025 results. The company has found more than one niche in which to thrive, and it is clearly effective at selling to those segments. Meanwhile, the medical device market is set to grow robustly, as the population ages and technology improves medical care. I'd consider buying Tactile's stock.

title: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary
content:
Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. Q4 2025 Earnings Call Summary - Moby

Strategic Execution and Commercial Momentum

Performance overperformance in Q4 was driven by the simultaneous maturation of investments in sales personnel, CRM technology, and refined go-to-market processes. The sales force reached a strategic 1:1 ratio of account managers to product specialists, optimizing the balance between clinical selling and complex order support. Lymphedema growth was bolstered by the new National Coverage Determination (NCD), allowing eligible Medicare patients to access advanced Flexitouch pumps without first failing basic pump trials. Airway clearance revenue surged 66% year-over-year, attributed to prioritized placement agreements with top 10 respiratory DMEs and rising clinical awareness of bronchiectasis. The acquisition of LymphaTech marks a pivot from a product-centric company to a solutions leader, adding 3D digital scanning to replace variable manual measurement methods. Management successfully retired the company's term loan and executed significant stock repurchases, reflecting a shift toward durable cash flow generation and capital flexibility.

2026 Outlook and Innovation Roadmap

2026 revenue guidance of $357 million to $365 million incorporates a conservative buffer for potential short-term administrative friction from new Medicare prior authorization rules. Management expects to launch a next-generation AffloVest in late 2026, featuring reduced weight, digital connectivity, and improved sizing to drive adherence. The product roadmap for Flexitouch includes phased updates to make devices smaller and lighter with less external hosing to improve the patient experience. AI-enabled technology will be expanded across the entire order lifecycle in 2026 to automate medical record reviews and accelerate speed-to-therapy. Guidance assumes airway clearance will continue to grow modestly faster than the lymphedema business as market penetration deepens within DME partner accounts.

Regulatory Shifts and Integration Risks

CMS announced new prior authorization requirements for pneumatic compression devices effective April 13, 2026, which may temporarily elongate the Medicare order cycle. The LymphaTech acquisition includes a separate R&D roadmap that management must integrate without compromising the effectiveness of existing clinical therapies. Operating expenses are projected to increase 8% to 10% in 2026 as the company annualizes the costs of the expanded 2025 sales organization. Clinical evidence for head and neck lymphedema is being leveraged to challenge 'experimental' designations by commercial payers to unlock further market access.

Story Continues

Q&A Session Highlights

Our analysts just identified a stock with the potential to be the next Nvidia. Tell us how you invest and we'll show you why it's our #1 pick. Tap here.

Impact of Medicare prior authorization on 2026 growth trajectory

Management characterized the new requirement as a short-term administrative headwind rather than a fundamental change to market demand or clinical eligibility. The company is leveraging its experience with commercial prior authorizations and new AI tools to minimize the impact on patient turnaround times. Guidance was described as 'balanced,' with the potential for upward adjustments in the second half of the year as the Medicare review process stabilizes.

Commercialization strategy for the newly acquired LymphaTech technology

The technology is currently used by garment manufacturers for fitting, but Tactile plans to integrate it into their direct sales bag following a strategic segmentation analysis. The tool is expected to be a 'no-touch' differentiator in high-volume clinics, providing objective 3D visuals that improve patient engagement and diagnostic accuracy.

Synergies between airway clearance therapy and new bronchiectasis drugs

Management views pharmaceutical entrants as a tailwind that increases general disease awareness among pulmonologists. Mechanical airway clearance and drug therapies are viewed as complementary, as drugs reduce inflammation while devices are required to physically clear mucus.

One stock. Nvidia-level potential. 30M+ investors trust Moby to find it first. Get the pick. Tap here.

title: Tactile Medical expects 8%-11% revenue growth in 2026 while expanding integrated lymphedema solutions
content:
Earnings Call Insights: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. (TCMD) Q4 2025

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* CEO Sheri Dodd highlighted 2025 as a pivotal year, stating that "Through disciplined execution across our commercial and operational strategies, we delivered strong profitable revenue growth while continuing to make critical investments in people and workflow-related processes." She reported total revenue for the full year of $329.5 million, a 12% increase year-over-year, and full-year gross margins of 75.9%. Dodd detailed the repayment of $26.3 million in term loans and $26.5 million in stock repurchases, ending the year with $83.4 million in cash and operating cash flow close to $43 million. 
 	* Dodd announced the acquisition of LymphaTech, describing it as "an exciting milestone in Tactile's evolution from a product-based company to a comprehensive integrated lymphedema solutions leader," and emphasized its potential for accelerating accurate disease identification and therapy access.
 	* The company’s 2026 revenue guidance is set at $357 million to $365 million, representing 8%-11% year-over-year growth. Dodd mentioned this incorporates "a potential short-term market impact from the recently announced Medicare prior authorization requirement for pneumatic compression devices."
 	* CFO Elaine Birkemeyer stated, "Total revenue in the fourth quarter increased by $80 million or 21% to $103.6 million." She also noted, "gross margin was 78.2% of revenue compared to 75.2% in the fourth quarter of 2024."

OUTLOOK

 	* For 2026, management projects total revenue between $357 million and $365 million, with expected growth of 8%-11%. Both lymphedema and airway clearance businesses are expected to grow in a similar range, with airway clearance growing modestly faster.
 	* The guidance accounts for a new Medicare prior authorization requirement for pneumatic compression devices, starting April 13, which management expects will have a "temporary short-term impact across the broader lymphedema market."
 	* Birkemeyer explained, "We expect our GAAP gross margin to be approximately 76%, our GAAP operating expenses to increase 8% to 10% year-over-year... net interest income of approximately $3 million, a tax rate of 28% and a fully diluted weighted average share count of approximately 22 million to 23 million shares." Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be $49 million to $51 million in 2026.

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* Tactile Medical delivered Q4 revenue of $103.6 million, with lymphedema revenue at $89.5 million and airway clearance revenue at $14.1 million. Net income rose to $10.6 million, or $0.46 per diluted share. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $22 million.
 	* The company ended 2025 with $83.4 million in cash and no outstanding borrowings.
 	* Gross margin expanded to 78.2% in Q4, attributed to lower manufacturing costs and stronger collections.

Q&A

 	* Kyle Edward Winborne, Piper Sandler: Asked about drivers of Q4 lymphedema performance and the impact of NCD interpretation. Dodd responded that "multiple investments in people and processes and technology... really enabled the company to outperform expectations," citing strong CRM adoption, increased rep hiring, and modest tailwinds from Medicare patients moving directly to Flexitouch.
 	* Winborne further inquired about balancing headwinds from new Medicare requirements with tailwinds. Dodd said, "our outlook... incorporates a very balanced and thoughtful approach," noting readiness for prior authorizations and expectation of only a short-term headwind.
 	* Iseult McMahon, BTIG: Requested color on the LymphaTech acquisition and its commercialization. Dodd explained, "they already have a commercialized product," and detailed plans to integrate LymphaTech into Tactile’s commercial engine, while working on regulatory and reimbursement strategies.
 	* McMahon also asked what could drive the lower or upper end of guidance. Dodd said, "an 8% would be the prior auth is really challenging," but emphasized that underlying business fundamentals remain strong.
 	* Brandon Vazquez, William Blair: Queried how prior authorization might affect CMS patient process times. Birkemeyer described, "the process is you curate a package... and that is submitted," noting it could "elongate the process" but expressing confidence in adaptation and technology support. Vazquez also asked about EBITDA leverage, with Birkemeyer highlighting "really good discipline from a cost management perspective as well as starting to see benefit from those investments that we've been making."
 	* Benjamin Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked for clarification on CMS requirements and potential impact of bronchiectasis drug launches. Birkemeyer said, "we have a good understanding of what Medicare is looking for a successful claim," and Dodd acknowledged increased disease awareness and market growth as positive factors.

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts were generally positive, focusing on strong Q4 performance, the integration of LymphaTech, and balancing reimbursement headwinds. Questions sought clarity on operational readiness and the timeline for navigating Medicare changes, with a constructive tone.
 	* Management maintained a confident and composed tone, providing detailed responses and reiterating readiness for policy shifts. Dodd repeatedly emphasized the company’s preparedness, stating, "we are prepared, we will address this," and Birkemeyer expressed optimism around leveraging new technology for operational efficiency.
 	* Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management reflected heightened confidence, with less focus on past headwinds and more on execution and future strategy.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Guidance was raised from $317-$321 million for 2025 in Q3 to a new range of $357-$365 million for 2026, reflecting ongoing commercial momentum and strategic investments.
 	* Strategic focus shifted from recovering Medicare headwinds and sales rep expansion in Q3 to integration of digital diagnostics (LymphaTech) and adapting to Medicare prior authorization in Q4.
 	* Gross margin improved from 76% in Q3 to 78.2% in Q4, and adjusted EBITDA increased from $14.4 million in Q3 to $22 million in Q4.
 	* Analyst interest moved from sales force expansion and product adoption in Q3 to operational readiness for reimbursement changes and leveraging new acquisitions in Q4.
 	* Management’s confidence in guidance and strategic initiatives increased, while analyst questions became more forward-looking and integration-focused.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* The main challenge identified is the new Medicare prior authorization requirement for pneumatic compression devices, which could cause a temporary slowdown in market growth as the industry adjusts.
 	* Management’s mitigation strategy includes leveraging experience from commercial prior authorizations and deploying new AI-enabled technologies to improve workflow efficiency and response times.
 	* Dodd acknowledged, "we are prepared, we will address this," signaling confidence in navigating the transition.

FINAL TAKEAWAY

Management underscored strong financial performance, robust commercial execution, and continued investments in technology and workflow optimization as drivers for future growth. The acquisition of LymphaTech marks a strategic milestone, positioning Tactile Medical as a comprehensive solutions leader in lymphatic dysfunction. Despite acknowledging a temporary headwind from new Medicare prior authorization requirements, management remains confident in its ability to deliver growth in line with the market and sees multiple catalysts ahead to sustain momentum through 2026 and beyond.
